title: Coryphopteris pauciloba
intro: Coryphopteris pauciloba, also known as the Asian fern, is a species of flowering plant in the family Pteridaceae. Traditionally, Coryphopteris pauciloba has been used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever reduction, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic effects, making it a valuable plant for natural remedies. The bioactive constituents of Coryphopteris pauciloba include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Coryphopteris pauciloba is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and extracts, and is often consumed to alleviate symptoms of colds, fever, and digestive issues.
taxonomy: Coryphopteris pauciloba is a species of flowering plant in the family Polypodiaceae. The plant is native to tropical Asia and is a type of fern. It belongs to the genus Coryphopteris, which is a group of ferns that are characterized by their unique leaf shapes and growth habits. Coryphopteris pauciloba is a relatively rare species, and its classification is often studied by botanists and plant enthusiasts.
